Put Rule B first to capture ‘with’ + food cases ([12],[13],[15]) as N before any other rule can mislabel them.
Next use Rule C to mark all remaining ‘act’ cases as V, including [5].

Baseline chosen because V is the majority label, giving 8 correct and 7 wrong with no blanks. Ordering B then C then A maximizes accuracy: B first prevents C from mislabeling food-with cases as V; C before A ensures [5] (Noun1=act) is labeled V, not overridden by A; A last finishes the remaining like-cases. This order achieves the highest accuracy (all 11 labeled correctly).
